event_hint: hottest March on record for the Lower 48 — YY's forest is experiencing an oppressive heat wave; the canopy isn't blocking anything, bark is warm before noon, the usual foraging line feels twice as long
notes: heat as equalizer — both paths pay a physical cost, but the gap between them widens because preparation and territory knowledge aren't equal
author_intent: forcing main and alt1-on-time to actually diverge in their stories — concrete narrative divergence required on day 7, not hedged convergence

branch_context.main: YY runs the west line in the worst of the midday heat, comes back with less than usual; sitting in nearest shade the east boundary is visible; the grey squirrel's offer not taken two days ago sits differently when thin and the air won't move — no decision yet, but the weight has a shape now
branch_context.alt1-on-time: YY doesn't run the west spot — uses west terrain knowledge from the ranging to find shade corridors that weren't on any mental map before; eats a little, moves carefully, loses less than expected; the hazelnut stays in inventory; the west is now a place YY knows how to be in
